metadata.title: Pandabox — Fund what's worth funding. On Sui.
metadata.description: A programmable funding launchpad on Sui. Launch a project, set the rate, run an on-chain sale — every parameter, every transaction, on-chain.
metadata.ogImageAlt: Pandabox — programmable funding launchpad for Sui.

wallet.pickerDescription: Pick a wallet to sign in. We never hold custody — your keys stay in your wallet.
wallet.noWallets: No Sui wallets detected in this browser.

pulse.flatlinedCaption: No payment events on this route. Pandabox cannot render a pulse for a page that does not exist.

notFound.headline: No signal on this route.
notFound.body: Pandabox couldn't find what you were looking for. Either the URL drifted between cycles, or this page was never on-chain to begin with. Pick one of the live routes below — they're all paying out signal.

hero.tagline: Pandabox is the programmable funding platform for Sui. Launch a project in minutes. Receive SUI, issue tokens, define payouts, all on-chain, all transparent, all yours.

finalCta.subhead: Configure cycles, payouts, and tokens. Sign one Sui transaction. You hold the admin cap.

empty.label: No projects
empty.heading: Listening for the first project on-chain.
empty.description: ProjectCreated events from pandabox::project will show up here.

howItWorks.title: Three steps from idea to on-chain funding.
howItWorks.subtitle: Every parameter you configure here becomes a Move call. Every interaction your supporters take becomes a transaction. Pandabox is what's between.

0.heading: Deploy
0.body: Configure cycles, payouts, tokens, and optional NFT tiers. Sign one Sui transaction. Your project goes live with an admin cap object you own.

1.heading: Receive
1.body: Supporters pay SUI directly to your treasury. They receive project tokens at your cycle's weight, plus tier NFTs if you defined any.

2.heading: Reconfigure
2.body: Propose changes for the next cycle. After the ballot delay, the new parameters lock in. Holders can cash out surplus at any time.

whySui.title: Built for the chain that's built for this.
whySui.subtitle: Three things Sui does natively that make Pandabox feel different in the hand — sub-cent payments, real ownership of admin rights, and zero-friction onboarding.

gas.heading: Sub-cent gas
gas.body: Supporters don't pay $30 to back a project. Pandabox payments cost fractions of a cent — the small contributions that matter actually go through.

ownership.heading: Object-centric ownership
ownership.body: Your ProjectAdminCap is a real Sui object. Transfer it to a multisig, hand it to a DAO, lock it in escrow — it's just an object.

onboarding.heading: Sponsored transactions
onboarding.body: Onboard supporters who don't yet hold SUI. Pandabox can sponsor the gas, so a first-time wallet can back a project on its first interaction.

theNumbers.title: Built on real on-chain data.
theNumbers.subtitle: Every figure here is computed from events on the Pandabox Move package, refreshed every 60 seconds.
theNumbers.sourceFootnote: source · pandabox::project events · sum / count / set
theNumbers.totalRaisedHint: sum of sold ÷ base_rate, all projects

tools.title: More than a launchpad. A toolkit for everything around the raise.
tools.subtitle: Pandabox ships utilities that pair with your project — bulk distributions, buyback pools, and more in the pipeline. All on-chain, all signed by you.
tools.futureBody: We're stacking utilities one at a time so each ships with the same care as the launchpad itself. Suggest one — we read everything.

tools.headline: A toolkit for the work around the raise.
tools.subtitle: Pandabox ships small, sharp utilities that sit next to your project. Bulk send tokens to a list of wallets, run a buyback pool against your treasury, automate the work that used to need a custom contract. Every tool is a Move call, every Move call is yours to sign.
tools.roadmapBody: Four more tools are queued behind the ones above. We're not ready to spec them publicly yet — but you'll see them land here one at a time, signed and on-chain, over the coming cycles. Watch this space.
tools.roadmapLockedAria: Upcoming tool — details not yet disclosed

sniper.title: Anti-snipe queue
sniper.body: Per-wallet rate limit on the opening seconds of a cycle. Stops bots, lets humans get a fair fill.

vesting.title: Linear vesting
vesting.body: Stream reserved tokens to team and partner addresses on a per-second schedule. Cancellable by the AdminCap.

splits.title: Multi-receiver splits
splits.body: Define a payout split once and route every cycle distribution through it. Hand it to a DAO if you outgrow it.

merkle.title: Merkle claim portal
merkle.body: Publish a root, let recipients claim on-chain at their own pace. Pairs naturally with the airdrop tool.

tools.suggestTitle: Need something Pandabox doesn't ship yet?
tools.suggestBody: Open an issue with a one-paragraph spec. We triage tool requests every Friday and the best ones become the next thing on this page.

airdrop.name: Airdrop
airdrop.tagline: Distribute SUI or any project token to thousands of wallets in a single signed transaction.
airdrop.description: Drop in a CSV, sign once, watch the recipients fill. Built for token launches, contributor rewards, and community drops where signing one payment at a time isn't an option.
airdrop.cap1: Bulk send · up to 10,000 addresses per tx
airdrop.cap3: Sponsored gas option for recipients with empty wallets

redeem.name: Redeem · Buybacks & Refunds
redeem.tagline: Devs set up buybacks or refunds of their project's coins. The deployment is permanent — no reversal, no admin override.
redeem.description: Two modes, one contract. Project managers can run a buyback pool that purchases tokens at a project-defined floor, or a refund pool that returns SUI to supporters at the rate they paid. Whichever you pick is permanent — once the pool is live, the terms are locked on-chain. No admin override. That's the trust guarantee holders see.
redeem.cap1: Two modes — buyback at floor, or refund at original rate
redeem.cap2: Permanent · once deployed, terms cannot be changed
redeem.cap3: Burn-and-receive in one Move call — no intermediary

redeem.headline: Cash coins out for SUI, on rules nobody can reverse.
redeem.subtitle: Devs deploy a permanent pool — buyback or refund — at a rate they fix at deploy. Holders redeem against the pool's SUI reserve in one signed transaction. Once it's live, the terms are locked on-chain. No admin override.
redeem.howTitle: Three on-chain calls. That's the whole protocol.
redeem.howStep1Body: Pick a coin, set a rate (mist per token), choose the recipient (burn or buyback), seed the reserve with SUI. One transaction, one shared pool object.
redeem.howStep2Body: Anyone can deposit more SUI into the reserve — typically the dev as redemptions happen. The pool's terms never change; only the depth changes.
redeem.howStep3Body: Holders burn (or transfer) their coins via the pool, receive SUI at the fixed rate minus the platform fee, all in a single signed transaction.

hero.pausedBody: Redeem and deposit are temporarily disabled by the platform admin. Existing pool state is unchanged.

about.title: About this pool
about.burnSummary: When a holder redeems, their {symbol} is routed to a burn address — the supply drops by exactly that amount. The pool's SUI reserve pays out the corresponding value at the fixed exchange rate.
about.buybackSummary: When a holder redeems, their {symbol} is routed to the project's recipient address for a buyback — circulating supply shrinks for the project, and the holder receives SUI at the fixed rate from the pool's reserve.
about.permanenceBody: The exchange rate (price_mist_per_token) and the recipient address were locked at deploy time. The contract has no setter for either — no admin can change them, no governance can override them. Only the depth of the SUI reserve changes as people redeem and deposit.
about.feeBody: Pandabox takes a {fee}% platform fee from the gross SUI on every redeem. The net SUI shown in the redeem panel already accounts for it.

activity.title: Activity
activity.beTheFirst: No redeems or deposits on this pool yet. Be the first.

redeemPanel.title: Redeem this pool
redeemPanel.feeDisclosure: {fee}% platform fee · taken from gross SUI · permanent terms
redeemPanel.successBody: Burned <amount/> from your wallet at the pool's fixed rate.

validation.exceedsReserve: Pool reserve too low for this amount

depositPanel.title: Top up reserve
depositPanel.body: Add more SUI to the pool's reserve so it can honour additional redeems. The pool's exchange rate and recipient don't change — only the depth.

notFound.headline: No pool at that address.
notFound.body: Either the object ID doesn't exist, or it isn't a Pandabox redeem pool. Double-check the address — Sui object IDs are 32 bytes (64 hex). The discovery surface only lists known-good pools.

page.metaDescription: Stand up a permanent on-chain redeem pool. Pick a coin, fix the rate, choose burn or buyback, fund the reserve, sign one transaction.
page.headline: Five steps. One signed transaction. No takebacks.
page.body: You'll pick the coin, set the exchange rate, choose where redeemed coins are routed, and seed the reserve. The contract shares the resulting RedeemPool<T> object — and from that moment, its rate and recipient are locked on-chain forever.

nav.resetBody: This clears every field in the wizard and your saved draft. The reset can't be undone.

coin.title: Pick the coin you want to redeem against.
coin.body: Paste its fully-qualified type, or pick from the coins you hold. We'll resolve its on-chain metadata and pin the decimals so the wizard math stays exact.
coin.errorNoMetadata: No CoinMetadata published for this type.

existingPool.eyebrow: A pool for this coin already exists
existingPool.title: This coin already has a redeem pool live on-chain.
existingPool.body: Multiple pools per coin type are allowed by the contract — you can still deploy your own with a different rate or recipient. Open the existing pool first if you just want to redeem against it.

rate.title: Set the exchange rate.
rate.body: How much SUI does one whole {symbol} redeem for? Once you deploy, the contract has no setter for this value  neither you nor a platform admin can change it.
rate.fieldHint: Whole SUI per whole token. Example: 0.001 means 1,000 tokens redeem for 1 SUI.
rate.validationTooSmall: Rate too small. The minimum price the contract can store is 1 mist (0.000000001 SUI) per whole token.
rate.permanentNote: Permanent — the contract has no setter for price_mist_per_token.

recipient.title: Where do redeemed coins go?
recipient.body: Pick where the contract routes the project coins users redeem. This is permanent — pick carefully.

burn.title: Burn
burn.body: Redeemed coins go to a known dead address (0x…dead). No private key holds it, so the coins are effectively destroyed — circulating supply decreases by exactly the redeemed amount.

buyback.title: Buyback
buyback.body: Redeemed coins flow to an address you control — typically your project's treasury or a multisig. The coins can be reused, re-distributed, or burned at your discretion later.

recipient.addressHint: 32-byte Sui address (64 hex chars after `0x`). Typically a multisig or treasury you control.
recipient.permanentNote: Permanent — the contract has no setter for recipient. Coins routed here cannot be recovered.

reserve.title: Seed the initial reserve.
reserve.body: The pool needs SUI on hand to pay out redeems. Anyone can deposit more later — this is just the launch depth.
reserve.fieldHint: Will be split from gas at deploy time.
reserve.gasWarning: Heads up — that leaves less than 0.1 SUI for gas. Deploy may still succeed; budget accordingly.
reserve.capacityNote: Capacity is an upper bound at the current rate. Each redeem settles at exactly the fixed rate; the reserve drops by the corresponding SUI plus the platform fee.

review.title: Review and deploy.
review.body: One signed transaction calls pool::create_pool, splits the SUI from gas, and shares the new pool object. After this, the terms are locked on-chain.
review.permanentHeadline: Once this signs, the terms are locked.
review.permanentItem3: No admin override. No platform reversal.
review.permanentItem4: Only the SUI reserve depth changes as people redeem and deposit.

success.headline: Your redeem pool is live on-chain.
success.body: The contract has shared a new RedeemPool<T> object. Its rate and recipient are now locked. Anyone can redeem against it; anyone can deposit more SUI into the reserve.

metadataOwner.title: This coin's metadata isn't freezable from your wallet.
metadataOwner.body: Sui only lets the owner of a `CoinMetadata` object reference it in a transaction. This coin's metadata is owned by another address, so the wallet's dry-run will reject any pool you try to deploy with it.
metadataOwner.hint: Ask the coin's deployer to freeze its metadata (`0x2::transfer::public_freeze_object`), or connect with the wallet that owns it.
metadataOwner.connectBody: We need to know your address before we can confirm whether the coin's metadata is referenceable from your wallet.

footer.blurb: The programmable funding platform for Sui. Built on-chain, open by default.

explore.title: Explore
explore.headline: Funded right now.
explore.subtitle: Every active token sale on Pandabox, read directly from the Sui mainnet package — no indexer in between.
explore.legacyHint: Deployed before 9-decimal scaling fix — numbers are unreliable.
explore.empty: No projects match these filters yet.

detail.legacyBody: This project was deployed before the wizard applied 9-decimal scaling to base_rate. The rate, target, and raised numbers below are off — don't trust them or back this sale.
detail.creator: Creator
detail.beFirstSupporter: be the first to back this project
detail.aboutEmpty: No description pinned yet — the creator can publish one any time. See the pay panel for sale mechanics.

meta.descriptionFallback: {name} ({ticker}) — on-chain funding on Sui, powered by Pandabox.

activity.title: Activity
activity.empty: No on-chain activity for this project yet. Contributions, claims, and withdrawals will surface here.

claim.claimIntroOne: The sale has closed. Burn your ContributionReceipt<T> object and receive your share of {ticker}.
claim.claimIntroMany: The sale has closed. Burn your ContributionReceipt<T> objects and receive your share of {ticker}.
claim.finalizeIntro: The sale end-time has passed. Anyone can finalize to lock in the final sold amount and unlock claim + withdraw for everyone.
claim.finalizePreviewIntro: The sale window has closed. Anyone can finalize to lock in the final sold amount — this unlocks token claims for supporters and SUI withdrawals for the project owner.
claim.finalizeFootnote: no-op if the sale isn't ready · gas only · sale becomes claimable for {ticker} holders
claim.finalizeSuccessBody: The project is now closed. Token claims and admin withdrawals are unlocked.
claim.tweetText: Just claimed my {ticker} from {projectName} on Pandabox — programmable on-chain funding on Sui.

share.tweetText: Backing {projectName} ({ticker}) on Pandabox — programmable on-chain funding on Sui.

rail.connectBody: Connect to back this project, claim tokens, or manage it if you're the creator.
rail.saleClosedBody: This sale is finalized. You don't hold a contribution receipt for {token}, so there's nothing to claim from this wallet.

seedLiquidity.title: Seed Cetus liquidity
seedLiquidity.intro: Marks this project as having paired liquidity on Cetus. Once saved, the price chart on the project page will start reading live OHLCV data from GeckoTerminal.
seedLiquidity.verifyExplainer: We verify your pool object on Sui before pinning. The pool must be a real pool::Pool<A, B> with one side SUI and the other your project's coin — {coin}. You can't flip the flag without an actual pool.
seedLiquidity.invalidObjectId: Not a valid Sui object ID
seedLiquidity.seededBody: The project page will pick up the new pool and start rendering the live chart within ~30s of cache revalidation.

pay.heading: Back this project
pay.inspectorPreview: Pre-sign preview. Your wallet will request a signature for this Move call.
pay.inspectorNotDeployed: Move package not deployed yet — submission is simulated locally until NEXT_PUBLIC_PACKAGE_ID is set.

page.metaDescription: Configure cycles, tokens, payouts, and tiers. Deploy to Sui.
page.headline: Configure your project, deploy in one transaction.
page.intro: Six steps. Drafts auto-save to your browser. The right rail shows exactly what supporters will see on your project page.

wizard.resetModalBody: This clears every field in the wizard and removes the saved draft from your browser. Nothing on-chain is affected — you just start from a blank form.
wizard.resetBullet1: identity, coin, sale, deploy — all wiped

imageUpload.errorTooLarge: Image too large ({size} MiB). Max {max} MiB.
imageUpload.dragOrClick: Drag an image or click to upload
imageUpload.sendingBytes: sending bytes to the pinning service
imageUpload.pinataReplicating: pinata is replicating across ipfs nodes — this can take a few seconds for large images
imageUpload.pasteCidToggle: or paste an existing CID / URL

step1.title: Identity
step1.body: Who is funding what. This is what supporters see in their wallet and on the project page.
step1.taglinePlaceholder: A photo-zine collective minting weekly drops on Sui.
step1.descriptionPlaceholder: Tell supporters what you're building and why their SUI matters.
step1.coverHint: Lands at the top of your project page. Wide 16:10 crops look best. Pinned to IPFS on upload — the CID goes on-chain.

step2.title: Coin
step2.body: Pandabox needs a Sui coin you own. You can publish one now in the browser using our audited template, or paste an existing TreasuryCap + CoinMetadata if you already have one.
step2.howThisWorksBody: We start from a pre-compiled coin_template.move with 9 decimals, rewrite its identifiers + metadata constants in your browser via @mysten/move-bytecode-template, then publish it from your wallet. The rendered Move source is pinned to IPFS so anyone can audit exactly what was deployed.
step2.name: Name
step2.iconUrlHint: Reuse your project cover or paste a hosted URL
step2.coinPublishedConfirm: Coin published. TreasuryCap + CoinMetadata captured to draft.
step2.publishCaption: one transaction · transferable upgrade cap returned to you
step2.errorNoDigest: Wallet returned no digest for the publish tx.
step2.strandedTitle: Published — but the RPC went quiet
step2.validateSymbol: 2–10 chars · A-Z, 0-9, _ · must start with a letter
step2.metadataHint: We read this from chain to detect the coin type + decimals.
step2.wrongDecimals: This coin is {decimals}-decimal. Pandabox requires {required} decimals — redeploy your coin with the correct value.
step2.pasteMetadataHint: Paste a CoinMetadata ID above to detect the coin.

step3.title: Sale terms
step3.body: Supporters contribute SUI in exchange for a Receipt. After the sale ends or sells out, they claim their token allocation. Anything unsold is burned or returned to you, per your choice.
step3.tokensPerSuiHint: How many {ticker} a supporter receives for each 1 SUI.
step3.targetRaiseHint: Editing this back-calculates Tokens per SUI.
step3.tokensForSaleHint: Total {ticker} reserved for this sale. The TreasuryCap mints up to this amount.
step3.supplyNote: The coin starts at 0 supply. Tokens are minted lazily when supporters call claim on their receipts. Final circulating supply = claimed
step3.unsoldBurnBody: Unsold tokens are permanently destroyed, reducing total supply.
step3.unsoldReturnBody: Unsold tokens are minted to your address. Use for vesting or DEX seeding.

step4.title: Review & deploy
step4.body: Pandabox pins your description and project_details to IPFS, then submits a single Sui transaction that creates the project, consumes your TreasuryCap, and transfers a ProjectAdminCap to your wallet.
step4.headsUpBody: Consumed on deploy: your TreasuryCap⟨T⟩ and CoinMetadata⟨T⟩ become owned by the Project object and can't be retrieved. Immutable on deploy: name, coin, base rate, allocation, end time, unsold-action. Editable later: description, icon, source-code blob, project_details (via update_metadata).
step4.sourceCodeHint: Pin your repo archive separately and paste the CID. Or leave blank and update later.
step4.deployCaption: consumes treasury cap + metadata · admin cap returned to you
step4.draftAutoSaves: Draft auto-saves to your browser as pandabox:draft:v3.
step4.inspectorIntroLive: Review what you're about to publish. Pandabox will pin your description and details to IPFS, then your wallet will sign one transaction that creates the project on Sui.
step4.inspectorIntroSimulated: Move package not configured — this submission will be simulated locally.
step4.youllKeepBody: a ProjectAdminCap in your wallet — your admin rights for this project. You can transfer it to a multisig later.
step4.youllGiveUpBody: your TreasuryCap and CoinMetadata. The project takes ownership and they can't be recovered.
step4.lockedOnDeployBody: name, coin, rate, allocation, end time, unsold-action. Description, icon and links stay editable.

empty.body: Spin up a token sale in minutes. Pre-deploy your coin, configure the sale terms, and ship in one signature.

supported.title: Projects you back
supported.subtitle: Your ContributionReceipts. Claim once the sale finalizes.

empty.label: Not backing anyone yet
empty.body: Back a project on Explore — your receipts and claimable share will surface here.

liquidity.noPoolLabel: No pool yet · chart in placeholder

info.whatBody: Every admin action — finalize, withdraw, update metadata, seed liquidity, transfer or renounce the cap — runs through the same on-chain entry functions as the project page. The buttons live in the panel on the right; pre-flight checks are surfaced inside each modal.

metadata.title: Docs
metadata.description: How Pandabox works on Sui: sale window, base rate & allocation, contribute & claim, unsold supply, finalize & withdraw, plus a wizard walkthrough.

docs.heading: How Pandabox actually works.
docs.subhead: Three tabs. Mechanics is the protocol; Walk the wizard is the create flow; Reference holds the glossary and FAQ.

how.label: 01 / How it works
how.p1: Pandabox is a Move package on Sui. A creator deploys a <term>Project</term> — a Sui object that owns a SUI treasury and a locked <term>TreasuryCap</term> for the project's own coin. The project then runs a single sale at a fixed rate: supporters contribute SUI and earn the right to claim project tokens at the configured price.
how.p2: Supporters don't receive tokens directly. They receive a <term>ContributionReceipt</term> — a transferable Sui NFT holding their entitlement. They <term>claim</term> their tokens by burning the receipt after the sale closes. Any payment that would exceed the remaining allocation is refunded in the same transaction.
how.p3: The sale ends in one of three ways: the configured end time arrives, the full allocation sells out, or the platform admin closes it. After close, the creator withdraws the raised SUI (minus a platform fee) and processes any unsold tokens per the policy they chose at deploy.

window.label: 02 / Sale window
window.p1: Every sale has a single locked window. You set an <term>end time</term> at deploy — or leave it open and close the sale manually as admin. Until the sale finalizes, anyone can contribute at the fixed rate.
window.p2: Three things finalize a sale: the end time elapses (<term>Time</term>), the allocation sells out (<term>Sellout</term>), or the admin force-closes it (<term>Admin</term>). Finalization is the gate for token claims, SUI withdrawal, and unsold-supply processing — none of those work while the sale is still active.
window.p3: Finalization can be triggered by the admin (try_finalize) or by anyone once the conditions are met (permissionless_finalize) — so a stalled creator can't trap supporters' claims after an end time has passed.

rate.label: 03 / Base rate & allocation
rate.p1: Two numbers govern the sale: the <term>base rate</term> (project tokens issued per 1 SUI) and the <term>funding allocation</term> (total project tokens to sell). Both are set at deploy and frozen for the life of the project — they can't be changed by metadata updates or admin actions.
rate.p2: If a contribution would push past the remaining allocation, only the affordable portion is accepted and the rest is refunded in the same transaction. Supporters never overpay.

claim.label: 04 / Contribute & claim
claim.p1: Contribute. A supporter calls contribute with a SUI coin. The project records the contribution against their address and returns a ContributionReceipt NFT — plus a refund coin if the payment exceeded what could be allocated.
claim.p2: Claim. After the sale finalizes, the supporter burns their receipt with claim and receives the project tokens. Multiple receipts (from several contributions) can be burned together in one call via claim_multiple, returning a single merged coin.
claim.p3: Receipts are regular Sui objects: transferable, sellable on secondary markets, and they don't expire. Claims remain open as long as the project object lives.

unsold.label: 05 / Unsold supply
unsold.p1: If the sale closes with tokens left in the allocation, the creator runs process_unsold to execute the policy they picked at deploy:
unsold.p2: <term>Burn</term> — the unsold supply is destroyed, raising the implied price of every claimed token. Signals scarcity: the float is exactly what the sale raised.
unsold.p3: Transfer to creator — the unsold supply moves to the creator as a normal coin balance. Useful if you intend to seed liquidity, run a secondary sale, or hold a treasury position. The project page surfaces this as UNSOLD → CREATOR in the spec line so supporters see the policy before paying.

finalize.label: 06 / Finalize & withdraw
finalize.p1: Once finalized, the creator calls withdraw_sui to pull raised SUI from the project treasury. The platform fee (in basis points, set by Pandabox) is skimmed automatically — the creator receives the net amount, the fee accrues to the platform treasury. Withdrawals can be partial; the cap holder can withdraw the rest later.
finalize.p2: The ProjectAdminCap minted at deploy is a regular Sui object. It moves via transfer_project_admin (which emits the event the indexer relies on — prefer this over a raw object transfer) or destroys itself via renounce_project_admin, leaving the project permanently un-administered — useful for community projects that want to remove themselves from the creator's discretion.
finalize.p3: Project metadata — name, description, icon URL, source-code blob, and the off-chain details JSON (tagline, category, socials) — stays editable via update_metadata whether the sale is active or closed.

intro.label: The four steps
intro.p1: The create flow is four steps. Everything you fill in here lands either on-chain (sale params, name, blob CIDs) or in the project_details JSON pinned to IPFS (tagline, category, socials). A live preview on the right shows exactly what supporters will see.

identity.alt: Step 01 of the create wizard — identity fields next to a live project-page preview.
identity.title: Identity
identity.caption: Project name, ticker, category, tagline, cover image, description. The ticker shown here is informational — the real coin symbol comes from the CoinMetadata you connect in the next step.

coin.alt: Step 02 of the create wizard — connect or publish a Sui coin.
coin.title: Coin
coin.caption: Pandabox needs its own Sui coin. Publish one from a pre-compiled coin_template.move (rewritten to your identifiers in-browser, pinned to IPFS for auditability) — or paste an existing TreasuryCap + CoinMetadata if you already have one. Decimals must be 9.

sale.alt: Step 03 of the create wizard — sale terms: tokens-per-SUI rate, total allocation, end time, unsold policy.
sale.title: Sale terms
sale.caption: The four numbers that get locked at deploy: base rate (tokens per 1 SUI), total allocation, optional end time, and unsold action (burn or transfer to creator). The implied max raise is derived live as you type.

deploy.alt: Step 04 of the create wizard — review and deploy.
deploy.title: Review & deploy
deploy.caption: One Sui transaction creates the Project object, consumes your TreasuryCap + CoinMetadata into it, and returns a ProjectAdminCap to your wallet. The poppy notice spells out what becomes immutable on submit and what stays editable via update_metadata.

terms.project: A shared Sui object holding the SUI treasury, the locked TreasuryCap⟨T⟩, and the sale parameters.
terms.projectAdminCap: Owned Sui object granting admin rights: withdraw SUI, process unsold, update metadata, finalize, transfer, renounce.
terms.contributionReceipt: NFT minted on contribute. Burnable for the entitled tokens after finalize. Transferable like any Sui object.
terms.baseRate: Project tokens issued per 1 SUI of contribution. Frozen at deploy.
terms.fundingAllocation: Total project tokens to sell. Once reached, sellout triggers. Frozen at deploy.
terms.unsoldAction: Policy for tokens left at sale close: Burn, or Transfer to creator. Frozen at deploy.
terms.endTime: Optional sale end timestamp. If null, only admin close or sellout can finalize.
terms.finalize: Lock the sale. Required before claims, SUI withdrawal, or unsold processing. Permissionless once conditions are met.
terms.closeTrigger: Why the sale ended: Time, Sellout, or Admin. Recorded on-chain.
terms.platformFee: Basis-point cut of withdrawn SUI taken by Pandabox. Set by the platform admin, applied at withdraw_sui.

audited.a: Not yet. Pandabox is in testnet. A formal audit will land before the mainnet ramp.

editAfterDeploy.q: Can I edit my project after deploy?
editAfterDeploy.a: Yes for off-chain identity: name, description, cover image (icon URL), source-code blob, tagline, category, and social links all update via update_metadata at any time. No for sale economics: base rate, funding allocation, end time, and the unsold-supply policy are frozen at deploy. The coin's symbol and decimals are set by the coin module you deployed before Pandabox — those can't be changed here either.

gas.a: Every transaction is paid by the wallet that signs it. Create, contribute, claim, withdraw — all signer-paid. Sui gas is typically sub-cent at mainnet rates, so the practical cost to back a project is the SUI you contribute, not the gas.

neverClaims.q: What if a supporter never claims?
neverClaims.a: Receipts don't expire. The tokens stay locked in the project's TreasuryCap until the holder burns the receipt with claim. A receipt is a real Sui object, so it can also be transferred or sold to someone else who'll claim it.

transferCap.a: Yes — use transfer_project_admin so the event lands in the indexer. The cap doesn't carry any project tokens or raised SUI; it's purely the admin key. You can also call renounce_project_admin to destroy it permanently — the project then runs without any admin at all.

compromised.a: The platform admin can flag a project as Compromised when there's clear evidence of misuse (rug, impersonation, malware in claimed perks). It's a one-way moderation signal — the project page stays accessible so supporters can see the flag and act, but it warns clearly. It does not seize funds.

featured.q: How do I get listed on the landing's featured row?
featured.a: Featured is just sort by SUI raised, top three. Raise enough and you appear automatically.
